game_name: Arcanum: Of Steamworks and Magick Obscura

element_introduction_order.element: Character creation and class path selection
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Fundamentally defines all subsequent gameplay: player must choose between technology, magic, or balanced neutral path before encountering any substantive challenges
Core loop of traveling through isometric world, visiting locations, discovering quests and dialogue options

element_introduction_order.element: Quest systems with multiple solution paths
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn quests can be solved via combat, persuasion, thievery, or bribery—encourages experimentation and replayability
Recruiting AI-controlled companions; player must manage party alignment and moral choices that affect follower loyalty

element_introduction_order.first_appears: early gameplay (can be avoided via stealth/diplomacy)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Multiple combat systems available; poorly balanced per reviews, becomes increasingly frantic; consequences for violence affect party reputation
High magic aptitude causes technological item malfunctions; high technology aptitude blocks beneficial magic and reduces spell effectiveness—creates persistent opportunity cost
Encounters can be avoided entirely through stealth or dialogue checks, rewarding non-combat character builds

element_introduction_order.element: Specialized skill trees (spells, weapons, crafting, thievery, social)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Every level-up allows skill improvement; every fifth level grants bonus point; 64 total character points available for specialization

typical_relief_triggers: Reaching safe hub areas with NPCs
typical_tension_triggers: Combat initiations (poorly balanced, frantic per reviews)
Party moral conflicts causing follower departure
Technological/magical aptitude meter trade-offs limiting build flexibility
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Player-paced exploration with optional encounter avoidance; tension arises from combat framing (poorly balanced design creates friction) and moral party management; relief through quest completion and safe social interaction. Open-world structure allows player agency in tension timing.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Isometric perspective reveals optional locations and NPCs; player can explore continental mainland and three islands in non-linear fashion

difficulty_curve.early_game: Character creation establishes build viability; early quests introduce multiple solution approaches; stealth/diplomacy allow avoidance of early combat
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Follower recruitment adds party management complexity; aptitude meter penalties become felt as specialization increases; combat balance issues become more apparent
notable_spikes: Combat encounters (poorly balanced per reviews, described as frantic)
Party alignment conflicts when followers find player conduct morally objectionable

notable_level_patterns.description: Locations serve as quest hubs where multiple NPCs offer varied missions solvable through different methods (combat, persuasion, thievery, bribery)
Players navigate continental mainland and three islands in non-linear sequence; quests intentionally designed with multiple solutions to accommodate different character builds and playstyles

Stealth, diplomacy, and non-violence options exist in parallel to combat encounters, allowing technology-focused, magic-focused, or balanced characters to progress without forced specialization penalties
